meaning: n.偏移量,抵销,弥补,分支,平版印刷,胶印vt.弥补,抵销,用平版印刷vi.偏移,形成分支
examples: The decline in marriage has been offset by a rise in cohabitation.
Prices have risen in order to offset the increased cost of materials.
A moderation in food prices helped to offset the first increase in energy prices.
The disappointing export figures, however, were partly offset by strong investment data released the same day.
Reynolds started raising the price of premium brands to offset payments to the settlement.
